# Coordinated Vulnerability Disclosure Policy

> Public report intake for security issues: report in good faith, stay within scope, and expect a
> fast acknowledgment and triage path.

## What this covers

This policy covers the handling of good-faith vulnerability reports for the public disclosure
surface. It is the place to check before reporting anything that might affect confidentiality,
integrity, or availability.

## Scope

In scope are defects that could create a security impact on the service, its public routes, or the
data they expose or mutate.

Out of scope are routine support questions, feature requests, and reports that do not describe a
security effect.

## Safe-harbor

Good-faith research that follows this policy is treated as authorized security work. The expectation
is that the report is made responsibly, the issue is not used to harm users or systems, and the
reporter gives us a fair chance to respond before any public disclosure.

## Report channel

Send reports to the published security contact. That contact is the single intake path for the
policy and the triage runbook.

## Acknowledgment and triage SLA

We aim to acknowledge receipt within one business day and to complete initial triage within three
business days.

## Signing

RFC 9116 signing is recommended, but deferred for now.
